title: woodsia pusilla var. glandulosa
intro: Woodsia pusilla var. glandulosa, also known as the glandular woodsia or glandular cliff brake, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Appalachian Mountains in North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Woodsia pusilla var. glandulosa are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water or using a decoction to create a medicinal tea, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
